Transportation and Mobility Research
Facilities

Power Electronics & Electric Machines Facilities
Facilities
NLR's state-of-the-art transportation and mobility research facilities provide a venue for groundbreaking innovations and collaboration with partners.
Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Research Facilities
These facilities encompass a wide range of evaluation platform capabilities, leveraging energy system integration and validation.
Energy Storage Facilities
These facilities help component developers and manufacturers improve battery and energy storage system designs.
Infrastructure Perception and Control Laboratory
The lab applies advanced sensing and computational controls to the coordinated movement of vehicles and people.
Power Electronics and Electric Machines Facilities
These facilities feature a range of equipment and modeling capabilities for advanced power electronics and electric machines experimentation.
Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Facilities
Hydrogen Infrastructure Testing and Research Facility
The facility consists of hydrogen, storage, compression, and dispensing capabilities for fuel cell vehicle fueling and component testing.
National Fuel Cell Technology Evaluation Center
The center plays a crucial role in independent, third-party analysis of hydrogen fuel cell technologies in real-world operation.
Safety Sensor Testing Laboratory
The lab aims to ensure that hydrogen sensor technology is available to meet end-user needs and to foster the proper use of sensors.
